items.title: Key → hash → slot
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  K["key 'alice'"] --> H["hash('alice') = 0xA1F3..."]
  H --> M["mod m (table size)"]
  M --> S["slot 4"]
  subgraph Buckets["buckets[0..m-1]"]
    direction TB
    B0["0: —"]
    B1["1: —"]
    B2["2: ('bob', 22)"]
    B3["3: —"]
    B4["4: ('alice', 30)"]
    B5["5: —"]
  end
  S --> B4
items.caption: The hash function selects a bucket; the bucket holds the (key, value) pair.

items.title: Collision resolution: chaining vs open addressing
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Chaining["Separate chaining"]
    direction TB
    C2["slot 2 → ('bob', 22) → ('eve', 41)"]
  end
  subgraph OpenAddressing["Open addressing (probe)"]
    direction TB
    O2["slot 2 = ('bob', 22)"]
    O3["slot 3 = ('eve', 41) — placed by probing"]
    O2 --> O3
  end
items.caption: Chaining keeps a list per slot; open addressing finds the next free slot — what CPython uses.

items.title: Resize when load factor crosses threshold
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A["n / m > 0.66"] --> B["allocate larger table m'"]
  B --> C["rehash every entry into new slots"]
  C --> D["swap in new table"]
  D --> E["future inserts back to O(1)"]
items.caption: One resize is O(n); spread over many inserts it averages to amortized O(1).
